children.text: The term “farmer” includes a person who is engaged in, or who, with assistance afforded under this chapter, intends to engage in, fish farming.
The term “farming” shall be deemed to include fish farming.

The term “owner-operator” shall include in the State of Hawaii the lessee-operator of real property in any case in which the Secretary determines that such real property cannot be acquired in fee simple by such lessee-operator, that adequate security is provided for the loan with respect to such real property for which such lessee-operator applies under this chapter, and that there is a reasonable probability of accomplishing the objectives and repayment of such loan.

The word “insure” as used in this chapter includes guarantee, which means to guarantee the payment of a loan originated, held, and serviced by a private financial agency or other lender approved by the Secretary.
The term “contract of insurance” includes a contract of guarantee.

The terms “United States” and “State” shall include each of the several States, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands of the United States, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and, to the extent the Secretary determines it to be feasible and appropriate, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands.
The term “joint operation” means a joint farming operation in which two or more farmers work together sharing equally or unequally land, labor, equipment, expenses, and income.
The term “beginning farmer or rancher” means such term as defined by the Secretary.
The term “direct loan” means a loan made or insured from funds in the account created by .section 1929 of this title

The term “farmer program loan” means a farm ownership loan (FO) under , operating loan (OL) under , soil and water loan (SW) under , emergency loan (EM) under , economic emergency loan (EE) under section 202 of the Emergency Agricultural Credit Adjustment Act (title II of Public Law 95-334), economic opportunity loan (EO) under the Economic Opportunity Act of 1961 (), softwood timber loan (ST) under section 1254 of the Food Security Act of 1985, or rural housing loan for farm service buildings (RHF) under .section 1923 of this titlesection 1942 of this titlesection 1924 of this titlesection 1961 of this title42 U.S.C. 2942section 1472 of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: who is eligible for assistance under this chapter;

children.label: B
children.text: who has not operated a farm or ranch, or who has operated a farm or ranch for not more than 10 years;

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of a cooperative, corporation, partnership, joint operation, or such other legal entity as the Secretary considers appropriate, who has members, stockholders, partners, joint operator, or owners who are all related to one another by blood or marriage;1So in original.1

children.label: aa
children.text: materially and substantially participates in the operation of the farm or ranch; and

children.label: bb
children.text: provides substantial day-to-day labor and management of the farm or ranch, consistent with the practices in the State or county in which the farm or ranch is located; or

children.label: aa
children.text: in the case of a loan made to a cooperative, corporation, partnership, joint operation, or such other legal entity as the Secretary considers appropriate, has members, stockholders, partners, joint operators, or owners, materially and substantially participate in the operation of the farm or ranch; and

children.label: bb
children.text: in the case of a loan made to a cooperative, corporation, partnership, joint operation, or other such legal entity as the Secretary considers appropriate, has members, stockholders, partners, or joint operators, all of whom are qualified beginning farmers or ranchers; and

children.label: aa
children.text: materially and substantially participate in the operation of the farm or ranch; and

children.label: bb
children.text: provide substantial day-to-day labor and management of the farm or ranch, consistent with the practices in the State or county in which the farm or ranch is located; or

children.label: aa
children.text: in the case of a loan made to a cooperative, corporation, partnership, joint operation, or such other legal entity as the Secretary considers appropriate, will have members, stockholders, partners, joint operators, or owners, materially and substantially participate in the operation of the farm or ranch; and

children.label: bb
children.text: in the case of a loan made to a cooperative, corporation, partnership, joint operation, or other such legal entity as the Secretary considers appropriate, has members, stockholders, partners, or joint operators, all of whom are qualified beginning farmers or ranchers;

children.label: E
children.text: who agrees to participate in such loan assessment, borrower training, and financial management programs as the Secretary may require;

children.label: F
children.text: who does not own land or who, directly or through interests in family farm corporations, owns land, the aggregate acreage of which does not exceed 30 percent of the average acreage of the farms or ranches, as the case may be, in the county in which the farm or ranch operations of the applicant are located, as reported in the most recent census of agriculture, except that this subparagraph shall not apply to a loan made or guaranteed under subchapter II; and

children.label: G
children.text: who demonstrates that the available resources of the applicant and spouse (if any) of the applicant are not sufficient to enable the applicant to continue farming or ranching on a viable scale.

children.label: i
children.text: writing down or writing off a loan under ;section 2001 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: compromising, adjusting, reducing, or charging-off a debt or claim under ;section 1981 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: paying a loss on a guaranteed loan under ; orsection 2005 of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: discharging a debt as a result of bankruptcy.

children.label: i
children.text: consolidation, rescheduling, reamortization, or deferral of a loan; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any write-down provided as part of a resolution of a discrimination complaint against the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: a city or town that has a population of greater than 50,000 inhabitants; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any urbanized area contiguous and adjacent to a city or town described in clause (i).

children.heading: .—Water and waste disposal grants and direct loans
children.label: B .—Water and waste disposal grants and direct loans
children.text: For the purpose of water and waste disposal grants and direct loans provided under paragraphs (1) and (2) of , the terms “rural” and “rural area” mean a city, town, or unincorporated area that has a population of no more than 10,000 inhabitants.section 1926(a) of this title

children.heading: .—Community facility loans and grants
children.label: C .—Community facility loans and grants
children.text: For the purpose of community facility direct loans and grants under paragraphs (1), (19), (20), and (21) of , the terms “rural” and “rural area” mean any area other than a city, town, or unincorporated area that has a population of greater than 20,000 inhabitants.section 1926(a) of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: has 2 points on its boundary that are at least 40 miles apart; and

children.label: bb
children.text: is not contiguous or adjacent to a city or town that has a population of greater than 150,000 inhabitants or an urbanized area of such city or town; and

children.label: II
children.text: an area within an urbanized area described in subparagraphs (A)(ii) and (F) that is within ¼-mile of a rural area described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Determination
children.label: ii .—Determination
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this paragraph, on the petition of a unit of local government in an area described in clause (i) or on the initiative of the Under Secretary for Rural Development, the Under Secretary may determine that a part of an area described in clause (i) is a rural area for the purposes of this paragraph, if the Under Secretary finds that the part is rural in character, as determined by the Under Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: not delegate the authority to carry out this subparagraph;

children.label: II
children.text: consult with the applicable rural development State or regional director of the Department of Agriculture and the governor of the respective State;

children.label: III
children.text: provide to the petitioner an opportunity to appeal to the Under Secretary a determination made under this subparagraph;

children.label: IV
children.text: release to the public notice of a petition filed or initiative of the Under Secretary under this subparagraph not later than 30 days after receipt of the petition or the commencement of the initiative, as appropriate;

children.label: V
children.text: make a determination under this subparagraph not less than 15 days, and not more than 60 days, after the release of the notice under subclause (IV);

children.label: VI
children.text: submit to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate an annual report on actions taken to carry out this subparagraph; and

children.label: VII
children.text: terminate a determination under this subparagraph that part of an area is a rural area on the date that data is available for the next decennial census conducted under .section 141(a) of title 13

children.heading: .—Exclusions
children.label: E .—Exclusions
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this paragraph, in determining which census blocks in an urbanized area are not in a rural area (as defined in this paragraph), the Secretary shall exclude any cluster of census blocks that would otherwise be considered not in a rural area only because the cluster is adjacent to not more than 2 census blocks that are otherwise considered not in a rural area under this paragraph.

children.label: aa
children.text: is a collection of census blocks that are contiguous to each other;

children.label: bb
children.text: has a housing density that the Secretary estimates is greater than 200 housing units per square mile; and

children.label: cc
children.text: is contiguous or adjacent to an existing boundary of a rural area; and

children.label: II
children.text: any urbanized area contiguous and adjacent to a city or town described in subparagraph (A)(i).

children.label: I
children.text: an area described in clause (i)(I) not to be a rural area for purposes of subparagraphs (A) and (C); and

children.label: II
children.text: an area described in clause (i)(II) not to be a rural area for purposes of subparagraph (C).

children.heading: .—Appeals
children.label: iii .—Appeals
children.text: A program applicant may appeal an estimate made under clause (i)(I) based on appropriate data for an area, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Hawaii and puerto rico
children.label: G .—Hawaii and puerto rico
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this paragraph, within the areas of the County of Honolulu, Hawaii, and the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Secretary may designate any part of the areas as a rural area if the Secretary determines that the part is not urban in character, other than any area included in the Honolulu Census Designated Place or the San Juan Census Designated Place.

children.heading: .—Exclusion of incarcerated populations
children.label: H .—Exclusion of incarcerated populations
children.text: Populations of individuals incarcerated on a long-term or regional basis shall not be included in determining whether an area is “rural” or a “rural area”.

children.heading: .—Limited exclusion of military base populations
children.label: I .—Limited exclusion of military base populations
children.text: The first 1,500 individuals who reside in housing located on a military base shall not be included in determining whether an area is “rural” or a “rural area”.

The term “borrower” means any farm borrower who has outstanding obligations to the Secretary under any farmer program loan, without regard to whether the loan has been accelerated, but does not include any farm borrower all of whose loans and accounts have been foreclosed on or liquidated, voluntarily or otherwise.
The term “loan service program” means, with respect to a farmer program borrower, a primary loan service program or a preservation loan service program.

children.label: A
children.text: loan consolidation, rescheduling, or reamortization;

children.label: B
children.text: interest rate reduction, including the use of the limited resource program;

children.label: C
children.text: loan restructuring, including deferral, set aside, or writing down of the principal or accumulated interest charges, or both, of the loan; or

children.label: D
children.text: any combination of actions described in subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C).

children.heading: .—Preservation loan service program
children.label: 4 .—Preservation loan service program
children.text: The term “preservation loan service program” means homestead retention as authorized under .section 2000 of this title
